content: I'll try to assist a bit. An IMAP account is one that reads and writes online to the server. This is ideal if you also like to access the account from another computer or from your phone. content:
Deleted mail goes to the Trash or Deleted folder (name depends on the provider.) Then, when you delete them from there, they are gone for good. Trash folder has a convenient right-click "Empty Trash" option.

To delete everything from a folder, select the folder in the folder list pane, press Ctrl+A to select all messages, then press the Delete key.

content:
Is this a POP3 account, rather than IMAP? If so, POP3 only downloads messages from your Inbox. If they are classified by the server and moved to a server-side folder, Thunderbird will never see them and will never download them.

Thunderbird's Spam folder is for messages that are downloaded locally and then flagged as Spam by Thunderbird.

content:
I think you are conflating address books and mailing lists.

Address books are discreet collections of contacts. Mailing list are sub list of that address book content that only contains links to the real records in the address book. If a person is not in the address book containing the list, then they will be added so the list has something to link to.

I just fiddled around and added a contact from another address book and the contact was added to the book with the list in it, so the software is working as it should. Nothing was removed from the original book, just a new entry was made in the current book containing the list to match the need to link to a contact in the book to include them in the mailing list.

You can not have a mailing list with discreet contact records any longer . That practice ceased a number of versions ago.
creator: MattAuSupport
